seo.title: Government RAG Pipeline AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for rag pipeline AI agents in government. Covers FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53.

content.intro: Deploying a RAG pipeline in a government context carries extraordinary stakes: a single hallucinated citation or unauthorized data exposure can trigger national security incidents, congressional oversight, or civil liberties violations. This checklist operationalizes FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53, and the Executive Order on AI requirements specifically for document retrieval and LLM synthesis architectures. Every item is designed to ensure that AI-assisted government decisions remain explainable, auditable, and constitutionally defensible.

sections.heading: Data Classification & Ingestion Controls
sections.description: Ensure only appropriately classified documents enter the RAG pipeline and that data boundaries are enforced before any vector embedding or retrieval occurs.

items.label: Classify all source documents before ingestion
items.description: Apply NIST 800-60 data categorization to every document entering the corpus. Tag each document with its classification level (Unclassified, CUI, Confidential, Secret) and reject any document exceeding the system's authorized classification ceiling before embedding.

items.label: Prohibit classified data from reaching external LLM APIs
items.description: Implement a hard technical control—not just a policy—that prevents any document tagged Confidential or above from being included in prompts sent to commercial or external LLM endpoints. Use an on-premises or FedRAMP High-authorized model for all classified workloads.

items.label: Enforce CUI handling procedures on vector store
items.description: Apply NARA CUI Registry controls to the vector database. Encrypt all embeddings at rest using FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules and restrict access to personnel with appropriate clearances and need-to-know.

items.label: Validate document provenance before ingestion
items.description: Verify the chain of custody for each source document using digital signatures or hash verification. Reject documents that cannot be traced to an authoritative government source (e.g., official .gov repositories, authenticated agency systems).

items.label: Implement data retention and purge schedules
items.description: Configure the vector store to automatically expire and purge document embeddings according to the source document's retention schedule under the Federal Records Act. Ensure purged documents are irrecoverable from vector indices.

items.label: Scan ingested documents for PII and PHI before embedding
items.description: Run automated PII/PHI detection (e.g., using NLP-based entity recognition) on all documents prior to embedding. Flag or redact Social Security Numbers, medical identifiers, and other sensitive personal data per Privacy Act requirements before they enter the retrieval corpus.

items.label: Maintain a complete, auditable document inventory
items.description: Keep a versioned manifest of every document in the RAG corpus, including ingestion timestamp, source system, classification level, and last-modified date. This inventory must be queryable for FISMA audits and incident response.

sections.heading: Retrieval Accuracy & Hallucination Prevention
sections.description: Implement technical safeguards within the retrieval and synthesis pipeline to ensure LLM outputs are grounded in retrieved documents and that fabricated or unsupported claims are detected and blocked.

items.label: Enforce source faithfulness scoring on every response
items.description: Integrate an automated faithfulness evaluator (e.g., NLI-based entailment model) that scores each LLM-generated sentence against the retrieved context chunks. Reject or flag responses where faithfulness score falls below a defined threshold (e.g., 0.85) before delivery to the end user.

items.label: Implement citation accuracy verification
items.description: After synthesis, programmatically verify that every cited document reference in the LLM output corresponds to an actual retrieved chunk from the vector search results. Block responses that cite documents not present in the retrieved context window.

items.label: Apply context relevance filtering before LLM synthesis
items.description: Score each retrieved chunk for semantic relevance to the query using a cross-encoder reranker. Exclude chunks with relevance scores below a calibrated threshold to prevent the LLM from synthesizing responses based on tangentially related or misleading context.

items.label: Set strict token limits on context assembly
items.description: Cap the number of retrieved chunks injected into the LLM prompt to prevent context dilution. Prioritize the highest-relevance chunks and document the selection logic so retrieval decisions are reproducible and auditable.

items.label: Deploy a hallucination detection layer post-synthesis
items.description: Run a secondary model or rule-based checker that identifies claims in the LLM output that are not supported by any retrieved document. Log all flagged responses and route them to human review rather than delivering them to citizens or decision-makers.

items.label: Require verbatim source excerpts alongside synthesized answers
items.description: For every AI-generated response, surface the exact text passages from source documents that support the answer. This enables human reviewers and citizens to verify claims directly against authoritative government records.

items.label: Test retrieval pipeline against adversarial queries quarterly
items.description: Conduct red-team exercises using adversarial prompts designed to elicit hallucinations, prompt injections, or retrieval of out-of-scope documents. Document findings and remediate identified failure modes before the next operational cycle.

sections.heading: Explainability, Auditability & Citizen Rights
sections.description: Ensure all AI-assisted decisions affecting citizens are explainable, documented, and subject to meaningful human appeal in compliance with the Executive Order on AI and agency-specific due process requirements.

items.label: Generate a decision audit trail for every AI-assisted output
items.description: Log the full retrieval context, ranked document list, relevance scores, LLM prompt, raw LLM response, and any post-processing steps for every query. Store logs in a tamper-evident, FISMA-compliant audit system with a minimum 3-year retention period.

items.label: Implement a citizen-facing explanation interface
items.description: For any AI output that informs a decision affecting a citizen (e.g., benefits eligibility, permit status), provide a plain-language explanation of what sources were used, what the AI concluded, and why. This interface must meet Section 508 accessibility standards.

items.label: Establish a formal appeal and human review process
items.description: Define and publish a documented process by which citizens can challenge AI-assisted decisions. Ensure a qualified human official reviews the original RAG output, source documents, and decision rationale before issuing a final determination.

items.label: Conduct an Algorithmic Impact Assessment before deployment
items.description: Complete a structured AIA per agency AI governance policy and the Executive Order on AI. Document the system's purpose, data sources, potential for disparate impact on protected classes, and mitigation measures. Obtain sign-off from the agency's AI governance board.

items.label: Assign a human-in-the-loop reviewer for high-stakes decisions
items.description: Identify decision categories (e.g., denial of benefits, security clearance flags) that require mandatory human review before any AI-assisted recommendation is acted upon. Document the reviewer's role, qualifications, and accountability in the system's ATO package.

items.label: Version-control all prompt templates and retrieval configurations
items.description: Store all system prompts, retrieval parameters, and reranking thresholds in a version-controlled repository. Tag each deployment version so that any past decision can be reproduced and audited using the exact configuration that was active at the time.

items.label: Publish a plain-language system card for the RAG pipeline
items.description: Create a public-facing system card describing the RAG pipeline's capabilities, limitations, data sources, and known failure modes. Post it on the agency's AI inventory per the Executive Order on AI transparency requirements.

sections.heading: Security, Access Control & FedRAMP Compliance
sections.description: Harden the RAG pipeline infrastructure against unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and adversarial manipulation in accordance with FedRAMP High, FISMA, and NIST 800-53 control families.

items.label: Obtain FedRAMP authorization for all cloud components
items.description: Ensure every cloud service in the RAG stack—vector database, LLM inference endpoint, object storage, and logging service—holds a current FedRAMP Moderate or High authorization appropriate to the data sensitivity level. Document the authorization boundary in the System Security Plan.

items.label: Implement Zero Trust access controls for the RAG pipeline
items.description: Apply NIST 800-207 Zero Trust principles: enforce identity verification for every API call between pipeline components, use short-lived credentials, and deny all access by default. Integrate with the agency's PIV/CAC authentication infrastructure for human users.

items.label: Encrypt all data in transit between pipeline components
items.description: Enforce TLS 1.3 with FIPS 140-2 validated cipher suites for all inter-component communication: query router to vector store, vector store to context assembler, context assembler to LLM endpoint, and LLM to response delivery layer.

items.label: Implement prompt injection detection and input sanitization
items.description: Deploy an input validation layer that detects and blocks prompt injection attempts before queries reach the retrieval engine. Log all injection attempts with source IP, user identity, and payload for incident response and insider threat analysis.

items.label: Apply NIST 800-53 AC and AU control families to all pipeline components
items.description: Map and implement Access Control (AC) and Audit and Accountability (AU) controls from NIST 800-53 Rev 5 to each pipeline component. Document control implementation in the System Security Plan and validate during annual FISMA assessment.

items.label: Conduct penetration testing on the RAG pipeline before ATO
items.description: Perform a full penetration test targeting RAG-specific attack vectors: data poisoning via document ingestion, embedding inversion attacks, indirect prompt injection through retrieved documents, and vector store enumeration. Remediate all critical and high findings before Authority to Operate.

items.label: Monitor for anomalous retrieval patterns in real time
items.description: Deploy a SIEM rule set that alerts on unusual query volumes, bulk document retrievals, off-hours access, or queries targeting documents outside a user's authorized scope. Route alerts to the agency SOC with a defined response playbook.

items.label: Establish a continuous monitoring program per FISMA requirements
items.description: Implement ongoing automated scanning for vulnerabilities, configuration drift, and control effectiveness across all RAG pipeline components. Report monthly to the ISSO and annually to the authorizing official as required by FISMA and OMB A-130.

notes.note: Every cloud-hosted component of the RAG pipeline—including vector databases and LLM inference endpoints—must operate within a FedRAMP-authorized boundary at the impact level matching the highest classification of data processed. The System Security Plan must explicitly document the RAG architecture's data flows, and the pipeline must undergo annual FISMA assessments with continuous monitoring reporting to the Authorizing Official.

The RAG pipeline must implement applicable controls from the SI (System and Information Integrity), AC (Access Control), AU (Audit and Accountability), and SC (System and Communications Protection) families. Particular attention is required for SI-7 (Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity) to address document corpus tampering, and SI-10 (Information Input Validation) to address prompt injection through user queries and retrieved documents.

notes.regulation: Executive Order on AI (EO 14110 and successor directives)
notes.note: The agency must complete an Algorithmic Impact Assessment and register the RAG system in the federal AI use case inventory before deployment. For any AI-assisted decision affecting the public, the agency must ensure the system is explainable, that citizens have a meaningful right to appeal, and that a human official retains final decision authority. The RAG pipeline's source attribution architecture directly supports these explainability mandates.

All citizen-facing interfaces that surface RAG-generated responses—including explanation pages, citation displays, and appeal portals—must conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards as required by Section 508. This includes screen-reader compatibility for source document citations, keyboard navigability for the appeal submission process, and sufficient color contrast for confidence indicators or warning labels on AI-generated content.

cta.heading: Automate Your Government Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your RAG pipeline against FedRAMP, FISMA, and Executive Order on AI requirements—catching hallucinations, access control gaps, and audit failures before they become congressional investigations.
